insight: On this Linux headless environment, the MuJoCo grasp quick start failed with MUJOCO_GL=osmesa but succeeded with MUJOCO_GL=egl in 1.26s, so backend guidance needs an explicit egl/osmesa matrix rather than a single default.

The existing evaluate_policy() in lerobot_plugin.py should be preserved as a generic API; LeRobot-specific integration should be an additive layer (evaluate_lerobot_policy()) that delegates to it, rather than wrapping eval.py or replacing the generic path.

The MuJoCo wedge currently validates and displays custom contract JSON, but runtime verdicts still come from hard-coded MUJOCO_GRASP_ASSERTIONS, so migration/custom thresholds can silently report PASS unless contract rules are compiled into the evaluator path.
